notes.note: The territory of Anagnia, a town of the Hernici in Latium on the Via Latina, southeast of Rome; Cicero is breaking his journey home at properties along the road. The "-inum" form names the estate or district rather than the town itself.

notes.anchor: on the fifth before the Kalends
notes.note: The Roman calendar counted backward (inclusively) from three fixed points each month: the Kalends (1st), the Nones (5th or 7th), and the Ides (13th or 15th). "The fifth before the Kalends" thus means the 28th of the preceding month by our reckoning.

Atticus had joked that wanting a fire lit first thing in the morning was a sign of old age; Cicero, quoting his Greek word for it, retorts that a failing memory is the more telling symptom. The exchange is light banter between two men in their sixties.
